message: Increased risk of severe liver toxicity/damage.
NSAIDs can reduce BP control and increase risk of kidney impairment.
Combined effect significantly lowers blood sugar; monitor for hypoglycemia.
Penicillins can slow down methotrexate clearance, leading to toxicity.
No major interaction. Safe to take, though spacing is often recommended.

Calcium prevents thyroid hormone absorption. Space by at least 4 hours.

Amlodipine increases statin levels. Simvastatin dose should not exceed 20mg.
Calcium in milk/curd binds to the antibiotic, reducing its effectiveness.
